Instability within the organization that you're following up on is going to cause this to be delayed. You have instability, you have in-house fighting, you have people not getting along, tearing each other apart, okay? Companies that are unstable internally cannot make a decision. Why? There's too much uncertainty, there's too much, I don't know, there's too many people and no leaders. So there's instability just hanging there. The company's not going away. They've been there for 20 years, let's say, or three or four or five years. The fact is every company goes through this where there's some instability going on for short periods of time. Hopefully it resolves, otherwise they'll go out of business and they're not going to buy anybody's product. Just be there. Stay there, okay? Be that consistent, stable person. Don't get caught up in the the backstabbing. Don't get caught up in the gossip. Stay away from that. Let them be in stable. You be the stable. Be the sane one. Be the one with common sense. Be the one that's scheduled and disciplined and always there, you know, like at your house on Thanksgiving. Somebody's got to have their brain still, you know, intact, okay? Don't be the unstable one. It's gonna happen just wetting the storm. You'll be there for the sale.
